questions.q: The arithmetic mean of a data set is given by which of the following formulae?
Which of the following is a disadvantage of median?
questions.answer: It is not suitable for qualitative data.

questions.q: Calculate the arithmetic mean of the following numbers: 10, 20, 30, 40 and 50.
Explain the concept of mode with an example.
questions.answer: Mode is the value that appears most frequently in a data set. For example, if we have a data set [1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 4, 4], then the mode is 4.

questions.q: What is the difference between mean, median and mode?
questions.answer: Mean is the average of all values in a data set. Median is the middle value when the data is arranged in ascending order. Mode is the value that appears most frequently in a data set.

questions.q: The following data represents the age of students in a class: 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20. Which measure of central tendency is most suitable for this data?
The mode of a data set is given by which of the following formulae?
Calculate the median of the following numbers: 10, 20, 30, 40 and 50.
Explain the concept of arithmetic mean with an example.
questions.answer: Arithmetic mean is the sum of all values in a data set divided by the number of values. For example, if we have a data set [10, 20, 30, 40, 50], then the mean is (10 + 20 + 30 + 40 + 50)/5 = 30.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of the median?
questions.answer: It is the middle value when the data is arranged in ascending order.
